summary: The Guernsey Registry issues a certified 'Statement of the Register' — a full extract of the publicly held electronic register (company name, registration number, type, status, registered office, incorporation date, and current/previous directors with service addresses and dates). This is the functional equivalent of a current register extract and is obtainable for a small fee (approx. GBP 10). It does NOT contain shareholders/members — Guernsey's register does not hold ownership data.

evidence.basis: Official Guernsey Registry — description of the Statement of the Register product
evidence.note: Guernsey Registry describes the Statement of the Register as a full certified extract of all publicly available information on the electronic register, including current and previous directors with service addresses and dates; a sample is published by the Registry. Basic company data is searchable free of charge.

evidence.basis: Appleby, Guide to Companies in Guernsey (Companies (Guernsey) Law 2008)
evidence.quote: The following information is available to the public from the Registry
evidence.note: The public information list covers memoranda, articles, resolutions and director changes, but excludes membership/shareholder details.

caveats: The Statement of the Register proves corporate existence, status and directors but NOT ownership; not a substitute for a shareholder document.
INFERENCE: exact field list taken from the Registry's own product description and published sample; the raw statutory list of publicly filed items was not retrieved verbatim.

summary: There is NO obtainable current-shareholder document from the Guernsey Registry for a company limited by shares. Under the Companies (Guernsey) Law 2008 the register of members is kept by the COMPANY at its registered office, not filed with the Registry. The annual validation filed with the Registry does NOT disclose shareholders (it covers directors, resident agent, business category, audit-exemption status and issued share capital — but not who the members are). Consequently shareholder identities cannot be retrieved from a Registry search. Beneficial-ownership data sits on a SEPARATE, non-public register. This applies to both private and public/listed companies limited by shares.

evidence.basis: Appleby, Guide to Companies in Guernsey — register of members
evidence.quote: Every company must keep registers containing specified details of its members, its directors, its resident agent, and, if appropriate, its secretary, which must be kept at the company's registered office.
evidence.note: Register of members is a company-held register at the registered office, not a Registry filing.

evidence.basis: Mourant, A guide to Guernsey's Companies Law — annual validation
evidence.quote: Guernsey companies are required to file an annual validation with the Registry between 1 June and 31 July each year containing basic company information
evidence.note: 'Basic company information' does not include a shareholder list; the annual validation does not disclose members.

evidence.basis: Appleby / Guernsey Registry — beneficial ownership register
evidence.quote: The content of the Guernsey Register of Beneficial Ownership of Legal Persons is not publicly available.
evidence.note: The separate beneficial-ownership register is non-public; obliged entities supervised in the Bailiwick can access it, others cannot.

caveats: CONFIRMED: shareholder data is NOT public because it is NOT filed at all — the annual validation does not require disclosure of shareholders. The current-shareholders document (register of members) is held PRIVATELY by the company.
The only route to a current shareholder list is the company's own register of members obtained directly from the company/registered office — not a Registry product.
Beneficial ownership register is separate and non-public — not a substitute.

INFERENCE: no distinction found between private and public companies as to Registry-held membership data; a listed company's shareholding may be visible via listing/market disclosure rules, not the Guernsey Registry.
UNVERIFIED: raw statutory section numbers not retrieved verbatim; facts corroborated by multiple professional guides and the Registry.

summary: A company limited by shares is constituted by its Memorandum and Articles of Incorporation, filed with the Registrar on incorporation and publicly available from the Guernsey Registry (approx. GBP 2). The memorandum shows the company name, type and objects; the articles govern internal management. This is the constitution/Gesellschaftsvertrag equivalent and is obtainable as a document.

evidence.basis: Guernsey Registry / Companies (Guernsey) Law 2008 — filing of memorandum and articles
evidence.note: The memorandum and articles of incorporation must be filed with the Registrar together with the incorporation fee and an application for incorporation; the Memorandum and Articles of Incorporation are publicly obtainable from the Registry (approx. GBP 2).

evidence.basis: Appleby, Guide to Companies in Guernsey — public information at the Registry
evidence.quote: The following information is available to the public from the Registry
evidence.note: Publicly available items include memoranda and articles and later special resolutions amending them.

caveats: The filed memorandum/articles are the constitution; they are NOT a current shareholder list and must not be treated as a Gesellschafterliste substitute.
INFERENCE: memorandum/articles description (fields, fee) from the Registry's product/legislation pages and professional guides; availability corroborated across sources.
